extraction.one_line_summary: VALVERDE Alejandro won stage 7 atop Mas de la Costa after sitting on QUINTANA's repeated accelerations, then exploiting LOPEZ's vulnerability in final 500m.
tactical_patterns: QUINTANA's repeated surges cracked VALVERDE at 500m out, but going too deep allowed VALVERDE to recover and counterpunch.
ROGLIC refused to lead on the concrete section with 300m remaining, forcing VALVERDE into track-sprint-style musical chairs positioning.
LOPEZ rode at his own rhythm instead of matching QUINTANA's accelerations, but paid when gradient kicked in final 500m.
UAE's SOLER drove hard early at the front of break, revealing team willing to sacrifice GC riders for stage hunts.

rider_signals.VALVERDE Alejandro: positive: at 38 years old, showed tactical maturity by appearing cracked at 500m then recovering to win from the front
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: set tempo and attacked repeatedly but burned matches too early, allowing VALVERDE to exploit his weakness in final 100m
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: negative: riding at constant rhythm instead of covering QUINTANA's surges left him isolated and dropped when gradient kicked hardest
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: refused to lead in final 300m positioning battle, suggesting he's calculating efforts across three weeks not fighting stages
rider_signals.VAN DER POEL Dylan: negative: dropped 5km from summit on only Cat-3 section, shockingly early collapse for red jersey wearer
